sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INTEGER, start_date DATE, end_date DATE);
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (1, DATE '1992-09-15', DATE '1992-11-14');
SELECT id, datesub('month', start_date, end_date) << 2 AS shifted_partition FROM test_dates WHERE shifted_partition IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, start_date, end_date, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_dates t2 WHERE t2.start_date < test_dates.end_date) << 1 AS boundary_count FROM test_dates;
CREATE TABLE test_numbers (num1 INTEGER, num2 INTEGER);
SELECT num1, num2, (datesub('month', DATE '2020-01-01', DATE '2020-01-31') << num1) AS complex_shift FROM test_numbers WHERE num1 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT num1, num2, (CASE WHEN num2 IS NOT NULL THEN datesub('month', DATE '2021-01-01', DATE '2021-02-28') << num2 ELSE 0 END) AS null_handling_test FROM test_numbers;
SELECT id, (1 << datesub('month', start_date, end_date)) AS bitwise_date_shift FROM test_dates HAVING bitwise_date_shift > 5;
SELECT (datesub('month', MAX(start_date), MIN(end_date))) << 3 AS extensive_shift FROM test_dates;
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE temp_dates AS SELECT start_date, end_date FROM test_dates;
SELECT start_date, end_date, (1 << (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM temp_dates)) AS temp_shift FROM temp_dates;
SELECT id, (datesub('month', start_date, DATE '1995-12-31') << 3) AS shifted_partition FROM test_dates WHERE start_date < DATE '1995-01-01' ORDER BY shifted_partition DESC;
